name: Month-End Financial Close
trigger: Last business day of each month
output: Board-ready P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow statement; variance commentary
steps: Export raw transaction data from Xero
Cross-reference Salesforce revenue records against Xero invoices
Manually reconcile any mismatches in Google Sheets
Categorize uncommitted expenses and accruals
Run payroll reconciliation against Rippling
Build management pack in Google Slides
Review draft with CFO (Sarah, acting CFO)
Submit final to board portal by day 5 of following month
pain_points: Manual Xero→Salesforce reconciliation takes 12+ hours alone
No single source of truth for revenue recognition — two competing spreadsheets
Payroll reconciliation requires manual Rippling export; no API connection
Variance commentary written from scratch every month; no template memory
Month-end often delayed by late expense submissions from Engineering
ai_opportunity_summary: High-ROI candidate for AI-assisted reconciliation. The Xero→Salesforce matching step is pattern-heavy and rule-based — a good fit for automated anomaly detection and auto-categorization. Variance commentary drafting is a strong LLM use case (structured input → narrative output). Estimated time savings: 15–20 hours/month. Kwame Asante (actor-020) is the primary beneficiary.

name: Customer Support Ticket Triage
output: Triaged ticket with priority, assigned CSM, estimated resolution time, and initial response sent
steps: Ticket arrives via Intercom (email, in-app, or Slack)
CSM manually reviews ticket content
Classify by type: bug, feature request, question, or escalation
Assign severity (P1–P4) based on account tier and business impact
Route to appropriate CSM based on account ownership
Send acknowledgment response to customer
If P1/P2: escalate to Engineering via Linear ticket
Log ticket in Salesforce against account record
pain_points: No automated classification — every ticket manually reviewed
Severity rubric lives in a Notion doc, not enforced
Routing to correct CSM requires tribal knowledge of account ownership
Duplicate tickets common — no deduplication step
Intercom→Salesforce sync has known data quality issues (Ben Osei owns the integration)
A previous AI pilot for this workflow failed in 2024 — team is wary
ai_opportunity_summary: Moderate-difficulty AI use case. Classification and initial routing are automatable, but the failed 2024 AI pilot (wrong tool, low adoption) means change management is as important as the technology. Recommend starting with AI-suggested triage labels rather than fully automated routing. Data quality in Intercom must be cleaned up first — fix Ben Osei's integration issues before layering AI on top.

name: Product Spec to Linear Ticket
trigger: Product spec document completed and approved in Notion
output: Structured Linear tickets (epics + stories) with acceptance criteria, estimates, and correct project assignment
steps: PM writes feature spec in Notion using standard template
Spec reviewed in weekly product review meeting
PM manually creates Epic in Linear
PM breaks spec into user stories and creates child tickets
PM assigns story points (requires Engineering input)
PM sets project, milestone, and assignee
Engineering lead reviews ticket set and adds technical notes
Tickets move to 'Ready for Sprint' queue
pain_points: Ticket creation from spec is repetitive and time-consuming
Acceptance criteria often inconsistent in format
Story point estimates require a synchronous meeting
Links between spec documents and Linear tickets break frequently
No automated check that spec template sections are completed
ai_opportunity_summary: Very strong AI candidate. Spec-to-ticket generation is a well-defined transformation task: structured Notion document → structured Linear ticket hierarchy. An LLM can draft tickets, generate acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, and suggest story breakdowns. This is one of the highest-leverage AI use cases in the product workflow — estimated 3 hours saved per spec cycle.

name: Engineering Code Review
output: Reviewed, approved, and merged pull request with passing CI
steps: Engineer opens PR against main branch
CI pipeline runs automatically (lint, tests, security scan)
PR auto-assigned to reviewer based on CODEOWNERS
Reviewer reads diff and leaves inline comments
Engineer addresses feedback
Second reviewer approves if changes are substantial
PR merged after 2 approvals and green CI
pain_points: Review queue builds up on Fridays — PRs sit unreviewed over weekend
No standard checklist — review quality is inconsistent
Large PRs (>500 lines) are rubber-stamped rather than thoroughly reviewed
Context on 'why' for a change often missing from PR descriptions
CODEOWNERS file has not been updated since 2023 — many orphaned areas
ai_opportunity_summary: Good candidate for AI-assisted first-pass review (automated comments on style, test coverage, and obvious issues). LLMs can also auto-generate PR summaries and flag large diffs for human attention. Reduces reviewer cognitive load without replacing human judgment. GitHub Copilot code review feature is a low-friction entry point.

name: Sales Handoff to Customer Success
output: CS team briefed on new customer; onboarding kickoff scheduled within 5 business days
steps: AE marks opportunity Closed Won in Salesforce
AE fills out internal handoff form in Notion (often skipped)
Salesforce automation triggers Slack notification to CS channel
CS manager assigns CSM to account
AE and CSM join 30-min handoff call
CSM creates customer folder in Notion
CSM sends kickoff scheduling email to customer
Onboarding kickoff call scheduled
pain_points: AEs frequently skip the handoff form — CS enters onboarding blind
Customer commitments made during sales cycle not systematically captured
No standard way to transfer deal context (pain points, champion, blockers)
CSM assignment is ad hoc — no workload balancing system
Time-to-kickoff varies wildly: 2 days to 3 weeks
ai_opportunity_summary: AI can auto-generate the handoff summary from Salesforce opportunity data, email threads, and call transcripts (if Gong is integrated). The commitment extraction step — identifying promises made during sales — is a particularly valuable LLM use case given Veridian's known gap here.

name: Customer Onboarding
output: Customer live on Veridian platform with configured workflows, trained admin users, and signed off on success criteria
steps: Kickoff call: align on success criteria and timeline
Technical setup: provision workspace, configure SSO if applicable
Data migration: import customer's existing workflow templates
Admin training session (2-hour call)
End-user training session
Parallel run period: customer uses both old and Veridian systems
Go-live sign-off from customer admin
30-day check-in scheduled
pain_points: SSO setup is manual and error-prone — no self-serve option (Hartfield commitment)
Data migration requires Engineering involvement for non-standard formats
Training content not updated since 2023; screenshots out of date
Success criteria defined inconsistently across accounts
Average onboarding time was 6 weeks before Wei Zhang was hired; now recovering to 4 weeks
ai_opportunity_summary: Medium-term AI opportunity. Near-term wins: auto-generate onboarding plan from customer profile, AI-powered chatbot for end-user training questions. Longer term: intelligent workflow migration tool that maps customer's existing processes to Veridian templates. Blocked by data quality and SSO infrastructure gaps. Wei Zhang's dedicated ownership (actor-019) is a prerequisite for any AI augmentation to land.

name: Data Pipeline Monitoring & Incident Response
trigger: Automated Datadog alert OR manual report of data anomaly
output: Incident resolved; root cause documented; postmortem filed in Notion
steps: Alert fires in Datadog or issue reported in #data-alerts Slack channel
On-call engineer acknowledges alert within 15 minutes
Initial triage: determine scope and affected downstream systems
Incident channel opened in Slack if P1/P2
Root cause investigation using Datadog and dbt logs
Fix deployed or pipeline paused to prevent data corruption
Downstream teams notified of resolution
Postmortem written and reviewed within 48 hours
pain_points: Runbook library is incomplete — 40% of incident types lack documented procedures
On-call rotation has been 3 engineers covering a 45-person engineering team
Root cause analysis often identifies same issues recurring — no feedback loop to prevent
Postmortem quality varies — no enforcement
Customer-facing data issues discovered by CSMs before Engineering (embarrassing)
ai_opportunity_summary: Strong candidate for AI-assisted incident classification and runbook suggestion. Given the known gap in runbook coverage, an LLM that can suggest investigative steps from alert context could significantly reduce MTTR. Postmortem generation from Slack thread and logs is a mature use case. Requires Datadog MCP integration.

name: Invoice Reconciliation
output: Reconciled invoice ledger; outstanding invoices flagged for follow-up; payment confirmations logged
steps: Export invoice report from Xero (PDF + CSV)
Cross-check against Salesforce closed-won dates and contract values
Identify invoices >30 days overdue
Draft chaser emails for overdue invoices (manual)
Update payment status in Xero when bank confirms receipt
Flag any discrepancies between contract value and invoiced amount
Weekly summary report to Nadia and Sarah
pain_points: Contract values in Salesforce frequently don't match Xero invoice amounts due to amendments
Overdue chaser emails written manually — no templates, inconsistent tone
No automated payment matching — bank feed requires manual reconciliation
Revenue recognition timing disputes between Sales and Finance on multi-year deals
Escalation path for disputed invoices unclear
ai_opportunity_summary: High-value AI target. Automated matching between Xero and Salesforce records, AI-drafted chaser emails with appropriate tone calibration by account tier, and anomaly flagging on contract vs. invoice discrepancies. Near-term win available with minimal infrastructure change — could be Veridian's first live AI workflow. Kwame Asante (actor-020) is the daily operator.

name: Engineering Sprint Planning
output: Committed sprint backlog with accepted tickets, capacity plan, and team assignments
steps: Engineering lead reviews 'Ready for Sprint' queue in Linear
Capacity check: confirm engineer availability for sprint
Priority stack-rank tickets with Product lead (Tom)
Engineers pull tickets and estimate in planning call
Sprint goal defined and written into Linear
Tickets assigned; sprint kicked off
Board synced to all team leads via Slack
pain_points: Ticket quality is inconsistent — many enter planning without clear acceptance criteria
Capacity calculation is manual (spreadsheet) and error-prone
Context from previous sprint retrospective not systematically surfaced
Tech debt tickets regularly deprioritized in favor of feature work (until Q1 2026 freeze)
Remote team timezone spread (London, Tokyo, Nairobi, Bangalore, Accra, Madrid) makes synchronous planning hard with 45 engineers across 5 squads
ai_opportunity_summary: AI can assist with capacity planning automation and ticket quality checks (flag tickets missing acceptance criteria). Sprint retrospective summarization and carry-forward context are strong LLM use cases. Does not require major infrastructure changes. Ownership of this workflow moved from Yuki to Oliver (actor-013) as part of the VP Engineering hire.

name: Quarterly Business Review (QBR) Prep
trigger: 6 weeks before end of each quarter
output: QBR deck; account health summaries; renewal risk register; roadmap update for top accounts
steps: CS pulls account health data from Salesforce and Looker
Product compiles roadmap progress against committed items
Finance prepares revenue and ARR summary by account
CS managers write narrative account health summaries (one per top-tier account)
Product identifies roadmap commitments at risk due to tech debt freeze
Draft deck assembled in Google Slides
Review with Sarah and Fatima
QBR calls conducted with top-10 accounts
pain_points: No single source of truth for account commitments — Chloe's personal Notion doc is the primary tracker
Product roadmap progress is manually compiled — no automated Linear → deck pipeline
Account health narratives written from scratch each quarter; prior QBR notes not surfaced
Finance ARR data often 1-2 weeks stale due to delayed close
Identifying which roadmap items were committed to which customer requires manual investigation
ai_opportunity_summary: AI-assisted QBR prep is a high-visibility, high-value use case — directly affects customer retention. Auto-generating account health summaries from Salesforce + Looker data, surfacing prior QBR commitments, and drafting roadmap progress narratives are all strong LLM applications. This workflow most directly illustrates the Company Graph value proposition.

name: Production Incident Response
trigger: P1/P2 alert from Datadog OR customer-reported outage
output: Incident resolved; customer communications sent; postmortem filed within 5 business days
steps: Alert acknowledged by on-call engineer (target: <5 min for P1)
Incident severity assessed; P1 pages Yuki and Marcus
Incident Slack channel created (#incident-YYYYMMDD-description)
Customer-facing status page updated within 15 minutes of P1 declaration
Root cause investigation begins; timeline documented in channel
Fix deployed and verified
All-clear posted to status page and affected customers notified by CS
Postmortem scheduled within 24 hours
pain_points: On-call coverage is thin — same 3–4 engineers rotated for 18 months
Customer communication drafting during incidents handled inconsistently
Postmortems filed >5 days late 60% of the time
No blameless postmortem culture — engineers avoid filing thorough postmortems
Action items from postmortems not tracked in Linear — they die in Notion
ai_opportunity_summary: AI can draft customer-facing incident communications in real time from technical Slack thread context. Postmortem generation from incident timeline is a well-documented LLM use case. Action item extraction and Linear ticket creation from postmortem text closes the loop that currently breaks.

name: Annual Strategic Planning
trigger: October 1 each year (target: plan finalized by December 1)
output: Annual company plan with OKRs, headcount plan, budget allocation, and product roadmap themes
steps: CEO sets strategic context doc (what changed, what didn't)
Each function head submits 3-year department vision
Finance builds revenue model scenarios (base/bull/bear)
HR models headcount plan against budget
Product and Engineering align on annual roadmap themes
Offsite: 2-day leadership team planning session
OKRs cascaded to team level
All-hands presentation of annual plan
pain_points: Prior year learnings not systematically surfaced — same debates recur annually
OKRs defined but not connected to workflows or commitments — orphaned shortly after all-hands
No mechanism to track which strategic decisions led to which outcomes
Department visions written in isolation — misalignments discovered late in process
Budget allocation process opaque to most team leads
ai_opportunity_summary: AI value here is in synthesis and institutional memory — surfacing prior year decisions, identifying recurring debates, and connecting OKRs to operational commitments. Not a near-term automation target, but a strong case for Company Graph: this is exactly the workflow that degrades without structured institutional memory.

name: Contract Renewal Management
trigger: 90 days before contract end date (Salesforce renewal alert)
output: Renewal signed or churn decision made; updated contract in Salesforce; finance notified
steps: Salesforce triggers 90-day renewal alert
CSM reviews account health: usage, NPS, open commitments
CSM prepares renewal business case (value delivered, roadmap items)
Commercial negotiation with customer
Legal review if terms change (Imogen Walsh, actor-016)
DocuSign sent and signed
Updated contract uploaded to Salesforce
Finance notified for revenue recognition update
pain_points: No structured view of open commitments per account — CSM must manually piece together
Usage data in Looker not always current at time of renewal prep
Renewal business case written from scratch each time — no template or prior version
Legal review turnaround time unpredictable (2 days to 3 weeks — Imogen Walsh is the bottleneck)
Commitment status for renewal accounts surfaced too late — some commitments discovered broken at renewal call
ai_opportunity_summary: High-value AI use case tied directly to revenue retention. AI can auto-generate renewal health summaries, surface all open and historical commitments per account, and draft the renewal business case narrative. Commitment status surfacing is the highest-ROI step — prevents surprise conversations at renewal calls.

name: New Business Sales Process
trigger: Qualified lead enters CRM (MQL threshold met)
output: Signed contract and Closed Won opportunity in Salesforce
steps: SDR qualifies inbound lead or outbound prospect
Discovery call: pain, process, budget, authority, timeline
Technical demo (AE + Carlos Mendez, Solutions Engineering)
Proposal generated from Salesforce CPQ
Security review if customer requires it (Carlos + Imogen Walsh)
Negotiation and legal review
DocuSign sent; contract signed
Opportunity marked Closed Won; handoff to CS initiated
pain_points: No formal process for capturing commitments made during sales — verbal agreements not logged
Proposal generation from CPQ is slow — custom pricing common for enterprise deals
Security review not standardized — Carlos answers same questions from scratch each time, no answer library
Legal review turnaround (Imogen Walsh) is the #1 deal-slippage driver according to Ravi Patel
AEs make product commitments to close deals that Engineering doesn't know about
ai_opportunity_summary: AI can assist with proposal personalization, security questionnaire auto-completion, and — most valuably — commitment extraction from sales call transcripts. The security questionnaire use case is immediately actionable: Carlos Mendez has enough institutional knowledge to seed an answer library that an LLM can query and draft from.

name: Cross-Functional Quarterly Planning
output: Agreed quarter priorities per function; cross-functional commitments documented; capacity conflicts resolved
steps: Fatima circulates planning template to all function heads
Each function submits top 5 priorities and resource requirements
Fatima identifies conflicts and dependencies
2-hour planning call with all function heads
Conflicts resolved; cross-functional commitments made
Summary doc published in Notion
OKRs updated to reflect Q priorities
pain_points: Commitments made in the planning call not systematically tracked
Prior quarter commitments not reviewed at start of next planning cycle
Cross-functional dependencies identified too late
Output doc in Notion is rarely referenced after week 1
No accountability mechanism for broken internal commitments
ai_opportunity_summary: The planning workflow itself is a human coordination problem, not an automation target. However, this is exactly the workflow that would benefit most from a Company Graph: surfacing prior commitments, tracking follow-through, and connecting cross-functional dependencies. The graph is the solution here, not an AI model.

name: Security & Compliance Review
trigger: Enterprise deal >£50K OR customer-initiated security questionnaire received
output: Completed security questionnaire; signed DPA/addendum if required; deal cleared for legal execution
steps: Sales (Carlos Mendez) flags security review requirement in Salesforce
Imogen Walsh assigned as legal reviewer
Customer security questionnaire received (usually PDF or web form)
Carlos and Imogen work through questionnaire collaboratively
Engineering consulted for technical architecture questions (typically James Okafor or Dev Anand)
Legal addendum drafted if terms require it
Completed questionnaire returned to customer
Deal cleared to proceed to commercial negotiation
pain_points: Each customer has a different questionnaire format — no standardization
No answer library: the same questions (data residency, encryption, access controls, SOC 2) are answered from scratch each time
Legal turnaround is the #1 reason deals slip according to Ravi Patel — ranges 2 days to 3 weeks
Engineering pulled in ad hoc with no scheduling — disrupts sprint work
No audit trail of previous answers — Carlos and Imogen hold institutional knowledge in their heads
ai_opportunity_summary: High-value AI use case with a clear starting point: build a security answer library from Veridian's previous questionnaire responses (Carlos Mendez has most of these), then use an LLM to draft responses to new questionnaires by matching questions to the library. Carlos estimates 60%+ of questions across customers are identical or near-identical. Reduces Imogen's review time significantly and removes Engineering from the critical path for most reviews.

name: Marketing Campaign Performance Review
output: Campaign performance summary; channel ROI analysis; recommendations for next month budget allocation
steps: Sophie pulls campaign data from HubSpot Marketing Hub
Sophie pulls pipeline and closed-won data from Salesforce
Attempt to join HubSpot lead source data with Salesforce opportunity data
Pull paid channel spend data (LinkedIn, Google) from respective ad platforms
Pull content analytics from HubSpot and Notion
Manually assemble in Google Sheets
Write narrative commentary
Present to Sarah and Fatima in monthly marketing review
pain_points: HubSpot and Salesforce are not properly synced — lead source data in HubSpot doesn't reliably join to closed deals in Salesforce
No attribution from campaign to closed deal — paid channel ROI is effectively unmeasurable
Content performance metrics scattered across HubSpot, Notion, and LinkedIn native analytics
Marketing-sourced pipeline is consistently understated because Sales reps don't update lead source in Salesforce
Report is assembled manually every month with no template memory — same structure built from scratch
ai_opportunity_summary: The attribution gap is a data infrastructure problem before it's an AI problem — the HubSpot-Salesforce sync must be fixed first (Ben Osei's work, tracked in com-014). Once data quality improves, AI can build a multi-touch attribution model and auto-generate the monthly performance narrative from structured inputs. Near-term win: AI-assisted narrative drafting from whatever data currently exists, even if attribution is incomplete.

name: New Employee Onboarding
trigger: New hire start date minus 5 business days
output: New employee provisioned, introduced to team, assigned buddy, and working through role-specific onboarding plan by end of week 1
steps: Anya Volkov notified of confirmed start date from Greenhouse
IT provisioning ticket created in Rippling (laptop, accounts, Slack)
Welcome email sent with first-week schedule
Buddy assigned (ad hoc, no matching system)
New hire completes Rippling onboarding checklist
HR orientation call (Anya or People Ops team member)
Manager check-in scheduled for end of week 1
30-day and 90-day review checkpoints created in Notion
pain_points: Onboarding documentation last fully updated in 2024; role-specific sections missing for most job families added since then
No role-specific onboarding paths — engineer onboarding is identical to CSM onboarding
Buddy assignment is ad hoc — Anya picks someone she knows is a good culture fit, no system
No measurement of new hire ramp time — unclear when someone is truly 'productive'
At 150 people and growing, ad hoc onboarding is creating inconsistent new hire experiences
ai_opportunity_summary: Medium-term AI opportunity. Near-term: use AI to generate role-specific onboarding paths from existing job family information in Rippling and Notion. Buddy matching could be improved with a simple algorithm (team overlap, tenure, interests). Longer term: AI-personalized ramp plan that adapts based on early feedback. The prerequisite is updating the base onboarding documentation first — AI cannot personalize content that doesn't exist.
